pages.title: Voix Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to Voix: what it is, why it exists, its design philosophy, and how the major subsystems relate. Links to child pages for architecture, configuration, security, and operations.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Installation prerequisites, build instructions, first-run setup, and how to migrate from sudo/doas. Covers toolchain requirements (Clang 22+, CMake, Ninja), dependency installation, and the sudo drop-in alias/symlink pattern.

pages.title: CLI Reference
pages.purpose: Complete reference for the voix command-line interface: all flags (-u, -n, -s, -i, -E, -l, -C), argument syntax, exit codes, and usage examples. Covers the 'incantation' invocation pattern.

pages.title: Core Architecture
pages.purpose: Overview of how Voix's internal modules fit together: the request pipeline from CLI parsing through permission checking, authentication, and command execution. Links to child pages for each major subsystem.

pages.title: Request Pipeline
pages.purpose: Step-by-step walkthrough of a privilege escalation request: argument parsing in main(), Voix::Voix initialization, catastrophic-command check, permission evaluation, PAM authentication, session management, and command dispatch.

pages.title: Permission Checker
pages.purpose: Deep dive into Voix::PermissionChecker: how ACL rules are loaded, how UID/GID identity is resolved at runtime, the first-match rule evaluation algorithm, command and argument filtering, and the deny-by-default principle.

pages.title: Command Execution Engine
pages.purpose: Internals of Voix::Command::execute: fork/exec lifecycle, signal blocking, privilege dropping (setuid/setgid/initgroups), environment sanitization whitelist, login-shell execution, file-descriptor closure, path resolution, and resource limits.

pages.title: Configuration System
pages.purpose: Overview of how Voix is configured via /etc/voix.conf, covering the YAML schema, the three top-level blocks (core, acl, security), and links to child pages for detailed ACL rules and security policies.

pages.title: voix.conf Schema and ACL Rules
pages.purpose: Full YAML schema reference for /etc/voix.conf: the core block (sanctuary, paths), the acl block (user and group realms, action/permit/deny, options flags: trust/nopass/keepenv/persist/nolog, target, command, args), annotated examples, and file security requirements.

pages.title: Configuration Loading and Validation
pages.purpose: How Config::load() works internally: secure-path validation (root ownership, no world/group write), YAML parsing with yaml-cpp, rule construction via parseRule(), identity resolution (getUidByName/getGidByName), regex blocklist compilation, and error handling.

pages.title: Security Subsystem
pages.purpose: Overview of Voix's layered security model: capability management, seccomp syscall filtering, path safety checks, catastrophic-command detection, and audit logging. Links to child pages for each security layer.

pages.title: Linux Capabilities and Privilege Management
pages.purpose: How Voix uses libcap for the raise-then-drop capability lifecycle: raiseCapabilities() (CAP_AUDIT_WRITE, CAP_DAC_READ_SEARCH, CAP_SETUID), dropCapabilities() with optional keep-set, the UniqueCap RAII wrapper, and how this integrates with the setuid binary model.

pages.title: Seccomp Syscall Filtering
pages.purpose: Architecture and implementation of the seccomp blacklist: the SCMP_ACT_ALLOW default policy, blocked syscalls (kexec_load, mount, ptrace, reboot, init_module, delete_module, etc.), the UniqueSeccomp RAII wrapper, integration point in Command::execute(), and the SECCOMP_PLAN design document.

pages.title: Path Safety and Catastrophic Command Detection
pages.purpose: How isSafePath() prevents directory traversal and access to forbidden paths (/etc/shadow, /etc/sudoers, /root, /etc/voix.conf). How isCatastrophicCommand() detects rm -rf / patterns and matches against the config blocklist. FileUtils::isSecurePath() ownership checks.

pages.title: Authentication (PAM Integration)
pages.purpose: How Voix delegates authentication to the Linux PAM framework: PamAuthenticator lifecycle, the pam_conversation callback, NOPASS/root bypass logic, pam_acct_mgmt account validation, session open/close, and the /etc/pam.d/voix configuration.

pages.title: PamAuthenticator and Session Management
pages.purpose: Detailed walkthrough of PamAuthenticator: constructor with Security shared_ptr and non_interactive flag, authenticate() decision tree (NOPASS → root bypass → pam_start → pam_authenticate → pam_acct_mgmt), openSession()/closeSession() lifecycle, and RAII pam_handle management.

pages.title: PAM Configuration (pam.d/voix)
pages.purpose: The /etc/pam.d/voix PAM stack: auth/account/password/session module stacking, system-auth delegation, pam_nologin.so enforcement, pam_limits.so session setup, and how to customize the PAM stack for different authentication requirements (e.g., MFA).

pages.title: Utility Modules
pages.purpose: Overview of the supporting utility classes used across Voix: FileUtils for filesystem operations, SystemUtils for user/group resolution, and Logger for audit logging. Links to child pages for each.

pages.title: FileUtils — Filesystem Abstraction
pages.purpose: API reference for Voix::FileUtils: fileExists(), isSecurePath() (root-ownership + no-world/group-write checks), readFile()/writeFile() with std::expected<T, FileError> error handling, resolveCommand() PATH search algorithm, and the FileError enum.

pages.title: SystemUtils and Logger
pages.purpose: Voix::SystemUtils for UID/GID resolution (getUidByName, getGidByName, getCurrentUser via getpwuid_r). Voix::Logger for dual-output audit logging to /var/log/voix.log and stderr, timestamp formatting with C++20 <chrono>/<format>, and log level conventions.

pages.title: Build System and CI/CD
pages.purpose: Overview of how Voix is built, tested, and released. Covers the CMake/Ninja build system, hardening flags, and the GitHub Actions pipelines. Links to child pages for build configuration and CI workflows.

pages.title: CMake Build Configuration
pages.purpose: Deep dive into CMakeLists.txt: voix_lib static library, voix executable, Clang toolchain requirement, C++26 standard, hardening flags (CFI, Shadow Call Stack, ThinLTO, ICF, stack protector, FORTIFY_SOURCE=3, PIE/ASLR), VOIX_ARCH variable, ENABLE_PERMISSIONS setuid install, and dependency linking (pam, libcap, libseccomp, yaml-cpp).

pages.title: CI/CD Pipelines
pages.purpose: GitHub Actions workflows: the release pipeline (tag-triggered build, LLVM 22 provisioning, packaging voix-x86_64-bin.tar.gz with binary/pam/conf/man/README) and the CodeQL security analysis workflow (weekly schedule, manual build mode, ccache strategy, security-events reporting).

pages.title: Testing
pages.purpose: How Voix is tested: the lightweight custom test framework (TestRunner, ASSERT_TRUE/ASSERT_EQUAL macros in test_assert.h), test_runner.cpp test cases (file existence, safe path, catastrophic command), BUILD_TESTING CMake flag, and the --run-tests CLI hook in main().

pages.title: Contributing and Community
pages.purpose: How to contribute to Voix: the Forge (build environment), Rituals (linting with clang-tidy, testing), code style conventions (CamelCase classes, lower_case functions, trailing-underscore private members), PR template, issue templates, security vulnerability reporting, and the Code of Conduct.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.purpose: Definitions of all codebase-specific terms, domain jargon, metaphors, abbreviations, and key class/function names that an onboarding engineer needs to know. Each entry includes file/class pointers.
